text.title: Agent Shape Decision Frame Procedure
text.summary: Five framing questions in binding order. Each step has typed input, action, and typed output.

step.name: frame-turn-count
step.action: Decide single-turn vs multi-turn. Single-turn = one input → one output; multi-turn = stateful conversation or iterative refinement.

step.name: frame-agent-count
step.action: Single-agent vs multi-agent. Multi-agent only if (a) tool count > 25 and bundling fails, OR (b) two distinct expertise domains with no shared context.
step.decision-gate: If agent_count == multi without one of (a) or (b), reject — multi-agent is the wrong shape.

step.name: frame-tool-surface
step.action: Classify tools as read-only, scratch-only (sandboxed), prod-mutating. Count each.

step.name: frame-deployment-surface
step.input: Where the agent runs: end-user CLI, server, scheduled job, SaaS embed.
step.action: If end-user CLI for coding/devops → hosted-only (Claude Code headless). Otherwise → custom.

step.name: frame-eval-surface
step.action: Identify whether a golden set / pass-rate threshold exists. Mark eval_available.
step.output: eval_available ∈ {true, false}. Compose final shape record per content/02-output-contract.xml.
step.decision-gate: If shape is multi-agent AND eval_available == false, escalate — multi-agent without evals fails.
